prompt:
Create a color variable set to pure green (00FF00). Draw a rectangle, give it a gradient fill, select the gradient's first stop, and bind that stop's color to the green variable using the variable affordance on the stop row (instead of picking a raw color).

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a color variable set to pure red (FF0000). Draw a rectangle, add a drop shadow via the Effects section "+", then bind the shadow's color to that variable using the variable affordance on the effect color row (instead of picking a color directly).

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the Local variables dialog from the left panel Variables tab, create a collection, and add a STRING variable named "body-font" with value "Roboto". Then create a text layer with any text, and in the Typography section use the variable affordance on the font family row to bind the font family to the "body-font" variable.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a text layer with the text "Tight crop". Open the Type settings popover via the sliders button in the Typography section, and in the Basics tab set the vertical trim to cap height.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the Local variables dialog from the left panel Variables tab, create a collection, and add a number (FLOAT) variable named "card-width" with value 240. Then draw a rectangle and bind its width field to the "card-width" variable using the field's variable affordance (or right-click on the width field) in the properties panel.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the Local variables dialog from the left panel Variables tab and create a new variable collection named "Theme" using the folder-plus button or the empty-state action. Inside that collection, use the Add menu to create a COLOR variable named "brand" with value pure magenta (#FF00FF). Then draw a rectangle and bind its fill to the "brand" token via the fill picker's variable tab.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the Local variables dialog from the left panel Variables tab, create a collection, then rename it to "Brand tokens" by double-clicking the collection tab (or via the collection menu).

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the Local variables dialog from the left panel Variables tab, create a collection, and use the Add menu to create a COLOR variable named "accent" with value pure yellow (#FFFF00). Then draw a square rectangle and bind its fill to the "accent" token via the fill picker's variable tab.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the Local variables dialog from the left panel Variables tab, create a collection, and use the Add menu to create a FLOAT (number) variable named "radius-lg" with value 24. Then draw a rectangle and bind its corner radius field to the "radius-lg" token via the field's variable affordance.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the Local variables dialog from the left panel Variables tab, create a collection, and use the Add menu to create a STRING variable named "cta-label" with value "Get started". Then add a text layer and bind its text content to the "cta-label" token.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the Local variables dialog from the left panel Variables tab, create a collection, and add a FLOAT variable named "gutter" with value 200 in the default mode. Draw a rectangle and bind its width to "gutter" (it should snap to 200). Back in the dialog, add a second mode named "Dark" next to the default mode using the "+" in the mode header, set "gutter" to 320 in the new Dark column, and make Dark the default mode via its column context menu so the bound rectangle follows the new mode.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the Local variables dialog from the left panel Variables tab, create a collection, and add a FLOAT variable named "panel-width" with value 240 in the default mode. Add a second mode via the mode header "+" and set "panel-width" to 480 in that new column. Draw a rectangle and bind its width to "panel-width" (it should snap to 240). Then open the second mode's context menu, rename it to "High contrast", and set it as the default mode — the bound rectangle width must jump to 480.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a COLOR variable named "surface" (any starting color) in the Local variables dialog and bind a new rectangle's fill to it. Then reopen the variables dialog and edit the "surface" value in its mode table cell to pure red (#FF0000).

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a small 32 by 32 square as an icon placeholder, select it, add an export setting, and set the export scale to 2x using the scale input's preset.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Click the Share button in the toolbar to open the Share dialog, then click "Copy link" to copy the file's link.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the Share dialog via the toolbar Share button, then click the audience row (the line describing who can access the file) to open the share settings sub-view.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the Share dialog, click the audience row to enter share settings, and change the audience dropdown from "Only invited people" to "Anyone", then click Save.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Copy the file's share link and open it in a second browser tab so a peer is present. In the first tab, click your own avatar in the toolbar to open the collaborators popover, open the peer row's "…" menu, and choose "Ask to spotlight".

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the file's share link in a second browser tab so a peer session is active, then move the mouse across the canvas in the second tab while watching the first tab's canvas.

Open the AI chat panel (right panel AI tab, or Cmd+J). In the provider selector, choose an acp: provider to connect the assistant to a locally running coding agent, and when the permission dialog appears, approve the agent's access so it can work on the file.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".
